fixes.issue: min_depth hardcoded to 2 in SnpCallingF64 dispatch
fixes.impact: GPU pre-filter now consistent with CPU post-filter thresholds

fixes.issue: compare_calls used exact position matching — 0/569 matches across 7 clones
fixes.fix: compare_calls_windowed() with ±5bp tolerance for coordinate representation differences
fixes.impact: Reveals true positive matches hidden by indel left-alignment and 0-vs-1-based offset

fixes.issue: No MAPQ, duplicate, or secondary alignment filtering
fixes.fix: PileupConfig gains min_mapq (set to 0 for sovereign mapper — see v3 finding), skip_duplicates=true, skip_secondary=true. Base quality filter min_base_quality=20 rejects Q2 instrument-flagged reads
fixes.impact: Base quality filter correctly rejects instrument-flagged junk reads. MAPQ filter disabled for sovereign mapper (v3 finding: ratio-based then gap-based MAPQ formulas both incompatible with FM-index multi-candidate scoring)

fixes.fix: SamRecord::is_duplicate() added, consumed by pileup filter
fixes.impact: PCR/optical duplicate reads no longer inflate pileup depth

mapq_finding: v3: Both ratio-based and gap-based MAPQ formulas produce MAPQ=0 for 97%+ of mapped reads. FM-index seeding + Smith-Waterman extension generates many candidates with near-identical scores even for uniquely-mapping regions. MAPQ filtering disabled (min_mapq=0) for sovereign mapper — base quality filter (Q20) provides the noise reduction. Proper MAPQ calibration requires a training set with known-correct mappings.

parity_evolution.note: 99.8% FP reduction; parity crossover (early over-call, late under-call); 0 position matches due to coordinate mismatch
Structural bugs fixed (GPU min_depth, MAPQ filter, dup removal, ±5bp matching). Re-run needed to measure updated parity.

MAPQ formula changed from ratio-based to gap-based. Finding: both formulas produce MAPQ=0 for 97%+ of mapped reads — FM-index multi-candidate scoring incompatible with standard MAPQ thresholds. min_mapq set to 0 (disabled). Base quality filter (Q20) retained — correctly rejects Q2 instrument-flagged reads. Variant counts now match v1 baseline (deterministic reproduction confirmed across interrupt/restart cycle).

results.note: Elevated variant count suggests hypermutator lineage
150bp reads: higher mapping rate, deeper coverage, more variants
Short 51bp reads: lowest coverage, very few variants, fastest processing
